herbs.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, has been used for centuries to create a natural, edible gum. This gum is made by boiling the roots of the plant and then collecting the resulting resin, which can be chewed or used as a sealant. The properties of yarrow gum have been studied for their potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.

Acorus calamus, commonly known as sweet flag or calamus, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine and as a natural sweetener due to its distinctive, sweet-tasting gum. The gum is derived from the rhizome of the plant and has been employed in various herbal remedies for its perceived health benefits, including digestive issues and stress relief. However, the safety and effectiveness of Acorus calamus gum are not extensively studied or universally accepted by modern medical standards.

Allium sativum, commonly known as garlic, has been found to have therapeutic properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be leveraged through its gum form. Allium sativum gum is a natural, non-perishable product derived from garlic that is often used in traditional medicine and as a dietary supplement. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential benefits of allium sativum gum for various health conditions.

Alpinia officinarum gum, also known as galangal gum, is a type of natural resin derived from the roots of the Alpinia officinarum plant, a species of ginger commonly used in Southeast Asian cuisine. This gum is valued for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, its commercial availability and uses may be limited due to the rarity of the plant and the need for proper harvesting and processing techniques.

Ammi visnaga gum, also known as ferulic gum, is a natural resin extracted from the seeds of the Ammi visnaga plant. Traditionally used in traditional medicine, it has been valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In the cosmetics industry, ammi visnaga gum is often used as a natural thickening agent and stabilizer in skincare products due to its emulsifying properties.

Apium graveolens, also known as celery seed, is a plant-based ingredient often used in herbal medicine and traditional remedies. The gum derived from Apium graveolens is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory properties and is sometimes used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. However, more research is needed to fully understand the benefits and potential interactions of Apium graveolens gum with other medications or health conditions.

Arctium lappa, also known as burdock, is a plant-based substance that has been used to create a natural gum. The gum, derived from the plant's roots, has been employed in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. While more research is needed to fully understand its effects, arctium lappa gum has been traditionally used in some cultures to support oral health and alleviate digestive issues.

Artemisia absinthium, commonly known as wormwood, is a plant-based substance that contains a resinous gum often used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies. The gum is extracted from the plant's roots or stems and is prized for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. Historically, wormwood gum has been used to treat digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.

There is no widely known information or products related to Atropa belladonna gum. However, I can provide information about Atropa belladonna, also known as deadly nightshade, a plant with toxic properties. It is not recommended to ingest or use any parts of the Atropa belladonna plant for medicinal or recreational purposes due to its highly toxic nature.

Azadirachta indica gum, also known as neem gum, is a natural adhesive derived from the sap of the neem tree (Azadirachta indica). It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries and is also employed as a bio-based adhesive in various industries, including paper and packaging. The gum has been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a promising alternative to synthetic adhesives in several applications.
